symptoms.symptom_name: Abrupt-onset, regular, very rapid palpitations (~185 bpm) at rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abrupt-onset, regular, very fast palpitations are characteristic of paroxysmal re-entrant SVT.
The abrupt onset and disproportionate rate at rest argue against sinus tachycardia.
A sustained, non-self-terminating episode needs a vagal manoeuvre and, if refractory, prompt medical termination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-syncope on standing, mild exertional dyspnoea, and neck pounding (cannon a-wave sensation) during tachycardia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Presyncope reflects reduced cardiac output at very high rates; the neck-pounding sensation (cannon a-waves) supports AV nodal re-entry, but without hypotension/syncope does not indicate instability.

symptoms.symptom_name: No crushing central chest pain and no syncope (haemodynamically stable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of crushing chest pain, syncope and hypotension indicates a currently stable SVT suitable for a vagal manoeuvre and urgent (not immediately emergent) termination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Heavy caffeine intake (two double espressos); no illicit stimulant use; no family history of sudden cardiac death

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Caffeine is a recognised trigger that lowers the arrhythmia threshold and fits re-entrant SVT.

symptoms.symptom_name: Regular narrow-complex tachycardia on 12-lead ECG captured in tachycardia (and post-conversion tracing)

investigation_details.expected_result: Regular narrow-complex tachycardia consistent with SVT; post-conversion tracing helps define the mechanism (e.g. AVNRT) and excludes pre-excitation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The arrhythmia mechanism is not captured and the diagnosis (and need for electrophysiology study) cannot be confirmed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The ECG captures the narrow-complex regular tachycardia defining the mechanism.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: An abrupt, regular, very fast tachycardia sustained ~45 minutes with presyncope but no instability indicates stable SVT - coach a vagal manoeuvre and arrange urgent ECG/adenosine, escalating if unstable.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Captures the rhythm mechanism; must be obtained urgently while the heart is racing.
Required for safe pharmacological termination (adenosine) and to detect any instability.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Holter monitoring and electrophysiology study/ablation referral
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: For definitive diagnosis and potential curative ablation of recurrent SVT, arranged after the acute episode.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance to wait it out at home without ECG capture
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: A sustained SVT should have the rhythm captured and terminated; waiting misses diagnosis and risks decompensation.
